title: add systemd to fedora image
body:
fedora removed the systemd package from its standard container image causing our systemd pid1 test to fail.

thanks to dwalsh for fix!

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

Should we cache a different image that already includes systemd to avoid
hitting the Fedora repos? Maybe ubi8-init?

@baude , your commit summary looks a little bit misleading: `add system to fedora image`

You are not adding `systemd` (I guess you made a typo) to the `fedora` image. What you are actually doing is replacing `fedora` by `ubi8-init` as the image you use for the "systemd PID1" test.

Perhaps a commit summary like this one would be more appropriate?
`test/e2e/systemd_test.go: replace fedora image with ubi8-init`

```
[+0287s] Caching registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8-init at /tmp/ubi8-init.tar...Running: /usr/bin/podman --root /tmp/crio --runroot /tmp/crio-run --runtime /usr/bin/crun --conmon /usr/libexec/podman/conmon --cni-config-dir /etc/cni/net.d --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --tmpdir /tmp --events-backend file --storage-driver=vfs pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8-init
[+0287s] Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8-init...
[+0287s]   A signature was required, but no signature exists
[+0287s] Error: unable to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8-init: Source image rejected: A signature was required, but no signature exists
```
As best I can tell, this is happening because there's a mysterious magical invisible `--signature-policy test/policy.json` in the `podman pull` command. The only possible place I see this happening is https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/test/e2e/common_test.go#L266 but although I see that being set, I don't see it being actually referenced anywhere.
Why was policy.json removed.  I updated my PR with this code but added back the policy.json change, and was able to pass the test locally.

@rhatdan that policy,json gets added to each system in CI.  I am not surprised that it passed locally at all.  With that policy.json though, you cannot pull anything from registry.access because it expects a signature and ... then are not signed or least not with a usable as-is signature.
Removing the signature falls back to Reject all though.

The story so far: what we need to do is remove this line: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/3d3359086ef0ed0833a8ac8e9d06d9934d835139/contrib/gate/Dockerfile#L30 and then (waves hands) rebuild the images and push them onto quay.

> The story so far: what we need to do is remove this line:
> 
> https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/3d3359086ef0ed0833a8ac8e9d06d9934d835139/contrib/gate/Dockerfile#L30
> and then (waves hands) rebuild the images and push them onto quay.

Currently pushing an image including this change. I'll restart the `in-podman*` PRs once the image is on Quay.

Woot!

For posterity: my comment above (re: gating Dockerfile) was incorrect. That should still be done, but it wasn't the cause of the CI failure. The correct solution, found by @vrothberg and @baude, was to manually rebuild and push the `in_podman:master` image.
